evals.prompt:
Write a GRI-Standards sustainability report from these survived findings. Organization: 'a global apparel manufacturer'. Include all required sections in the GRI frame and a GRI content index table. Write the report to sustainability-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_gri_1 | title: The organization operates 14 manufacturing sites across 6 countries with 22,000 employees | dimension: general-disclosures | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/org-profile | author: GRI 2 Disclosure | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_gri_2 | title: Stakeholder engagement identified water use and labor conditions as the two highest-priority material topics | dimension: materiality | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/materiality | author: GRI 3 Determination | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_gri_3 | title: Absolute Scope 1 and 2 emissions fell 11% year over year to 480,000 tonnes CO2e | dimension: environmental | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/emissions | author: GRI 305 | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_gri_4 | title: The reported gender pay gap narrowed to 4.2% across the global workforce | dimension: social | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/pay-gap | author: GRI 405 | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A GRI-structured sustainability report with sections in order: GRI 1 Foundation, GRI 2 General Disclosures, GRI 3 Material Topics, Topic Standards (GRI 200/300/400), and a GRI Content Index table. The Material Topics section presents the materiality determination. The content index maps disclosures to locations. Every claim traces to a finding. No raw urn:mif: identifiers in prose. Scope caveat stated (GRI-structured, not GRI-assured).

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Required GRI 1 Foundation section present
Required GRI 2 General Disclosures section present
Required GRI 3 Material Topics section present
Required GRI content index present (distinguishing back-matter)
A real markdown table (header separator row) appears under the GRI content index heading, not merely an inline pipe
Ordering: Material Topics precedes the GRI Content Index
At least one citation URL from the MIF findings appears as a reference
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose or body text
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The Material Topics section presents a materiality determination, not just a list
expectations.criteria: The GRI 3 Material Topics section explains how water use and labor conditions were identified and prioritized as material (e.g., via stakeholder engagement), rather than merely listing topics.

expectations.description: The GRI content index maps disclosures to their locations
expectations.criteria: The content index is a table associating GRI disclosure numbers (e.g., GRI 305 emissions, GRI 405 pay gap) with where they are reported and the finding behind them.

expectations.description: No raw urn:mif: identifiers or f_gri_ internal IDs appear as visible prose
expectations.criteria: The strings 'urn:mif:' and 'f_gri_' must not appear as visible body text; internal IDs resolve to disclosure-indexed references.

evals.prompt:
Produce a GRI-Standards sustainability report on 'an energy utility'. The finding set includes one falsified finding and one weakened finding — handle each according to the genre rules. Write the report to sustainability-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_util_1 | title: Renewable sources supplied 38% of generation capacity by year end | dimension: environmental | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/renewables-share | author: GRI 302 | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_util_2 | title: The utility is fully carbon neutral today across all scopes with no offsets required | dimension: environmental | verdict: falsified | citation: https://example.com/neutral-claim | author: Marketing | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_util_3 | title: A methane-leak reduction program may cut fugitive emissions materially, though monitoring data is preliminary | dimension: environmental | verdict: weakened | citation: https://example.com/methane-program | author: Pilot Report | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A GRI report that (1) excludes the falsified carbon-neutral claim (f_util_2), (2) includes the weakened methane program (f_util_3) with an explicit uncertainty qualifier, (3) uses the survived renewables share (f_util_1), (4) keeps GRI section ordering and includes a content index.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
Material Topics section present
GRI content index present
Survived finding f_util_1 (renewables share) appears in the report
Weakened finding f_util_3 (methane program) appears with annotation (weakened = include)
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the prose
No internal MIF finding IDs appear in the prose

expectations.description: The falsified carbon-neutral claim does NOT appear as a disclosure
expectations.criteria: The claim that the utility is fully carbon neutral today (Marketing 2025) must not appear as a reported fact. It may be referenced only when describing the verification/exclusion policy.

expectations.description: The weakened methane program carries an explicit uncertainty qualifier
expectations.criteria: The methane-leak reduction program is presented with a qualifier such as 'preliminary', 'pilot', or 'monitoring data is early' — not asserted as a realized emissions reduction.

evals.prompt:
Write a GRI-Standards sustainability report from these findings. Organization: 'a food and beverage company'. All four findings survived. Citations are mandatory; every claim must trace to a finding. Write the report to sustainability-report.md.

Findings:
- id: urn:mif:f_fb_1 | title: Governance of sustainability sits with a board-level committee meeting quarterly | dimension: general-disclosures | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/governance | author: GRI 2 | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_fb_2 | title: Materiality assessment ranked water stewardship and packaging waste as top topics | dimension: materiality | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/fb-materiality | author: GRI 3 | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_fb_3 | title: Water withdrawal intensity dropped 8% per tonne of product | dimension: environmental | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/water-intensity | author: GRI 303 | year: 2025
- id: urn:mif:f_fb_4 | title: Recyclable packaging reached 71% of total packaging by weight | dimension: environmental | verdict: survived | citation: https://example.com/packaging | author: GRI 301 | year: 2025
evals.expected_output: A complete GRI report with all sections in order and a GRI content index table. Every claim traces to one of the four findings with a disclosure-indexed reference. The Material Topics section reflects water stewardship and packaging waste. No uncited assertions. No urn:mif: leakage in prose.

deterministic_checks.description: Output file was created
GRI 1 Foundation section present
GRI 2 General Disclosures section present
GRI 3 Material Topics section present
GRI content index present
Ordering: Material Topics precedes the GRI Content Index
Finding f_fb_2 (material topics) referenced
Finding f_fb_4 (recyclable packaging) referenced
No raw urn:mif: identifiers appear in the body text
No internal MIF finding ID handles leak into the output body

expectations.description: Every claim traces to a supplied finding with a disclosure-indexed reference
expectations.criteria: Each disclosure cites the named GRI source from the findings (GRI 2, GRI 3, GRI 303, GRI 301) with its URL. No uncited disclosures.

expectations.description: The content index lists the topic-standard disclosures with locations
expectations.criteria: The GRI content index associates the topic-standard disclosures (e.g., GRI 303 water, GRI 301 materials) with where they appear and the finding behind each.
